annotations.label: Convex set (informal)

anchor.snippet: a set of points is convex if it contains every line segment between two points in the set

annotations.note: `Convex` with `convex_iff_segment_subset` states exactly that the segment between any two points of the set lies in the set.

annotations.label: Solid cube vs. crescent

anchor.snippet: a solid cube is a convex set, but anything that is hollow or has an indent

annotations.note: Informal illustrative example with no corresponding Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Boundary is a convex curve

anchor.snippet: The boundary of a convex set in the plane is always a convex curve

annotations.note: Mathlib has no theory of convex curves / boundaries of planar convex sets.

annotations.label: Convex hull

anchor.snippet: is called the convex hull of A

annotations.note: `convexHull` is the smallest convex set containing a given set, defined as a closure operator.

annotations.label: Convex function

anchor.snippet: A convex function is a real-valued function defined on an interval

annotations.note: `ConvexOn` defines convexity of a function on a convex domain via the midpoint inequality.

annotations.label: Convex set (formal)

anchor.snippet: A subset C of S is convex if, for all x and y in C

annotations.note: `Convex` is the formal predicate matching the textbook definition (see also `convex_iff_add_mem`).

annotations.label: Affine combination characterization

anchor.snippet: the affine combination (1 − t ) x + ty belongs to C

annotations.note: `convex_iff_add_mem` characterizes convexity by `a•x + b•y ∈ s` for `a,b ≥ 0`, `a+b = 1`.

annotations.label: Invariance under affine transformations

anchor.snippet: convexity is invariant under affine transformations

annotations.note: `Convex.affine_image` and `Convex.affine_preimage` show convexity is preserved by affine maps.

annotations.label: Convex sets are path-connected

anchor.snippet: is path-connected (and therefore also connected )

annotations.note: `Convex.isPathConnected` proves a nonempty convex set is path-connected.

annotations.label: Strictly convex set

anchor.snippet: A set C is strictly convex if for every x and y in C

annotations.note: `StrictConvex` requires open segments between distinct points to lie in the interior.

annotations.label: Strictly convex iff boundary points are extreme

anchor.snippet: is strictly convex if and only if every one of its boundary points is an extreme point

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the forward direction (strictly convex ⇒ boundary points are extreme) but not the full iff.

annotations.label: Absolutely convex set

anchor.snippet: A set C is absolutely convex if it is convex and balanced

annotations.note: `AbsConvex` is defined exactly as `Balanced 𝕜 s ∧ Convex 𝕜 s`.

annotations.label: Convex subsets of R

anchor.snippet: The convex subsets of R (the set of real numbers) are the intervals and the points of R

annotations.note: `convex_iff_ordConnected` shows convex subsets of a linearly ordered field are exactly the order-connected sets (intervals).

annotations.label: Convex subsets of the plane

anchor.snippet: convex subsets of the Euclidean plane are solid regular polygons

annotations.note: Specific planar geometric examples (regular polygons, etc.) are not formalized.

annotations.label: Convex subsets of 3-space

anchor.snippet: convex subsets of a Euclidean 3-dimensional space are the Archimedean solids and the Platonic solids

annotations.note: Archimedean and Platonic solids as convex examples are not formalized.

annotations.label: Kepler-Poinsot non-convex

anchor.snippet: The Kepler-Poinsot polyhedra are examples of non-convex sets

annotations.note: Kepler–Poinsot polyhedra are not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Non-convex set

anchor.snippet: A set that is not convex is called a non-convex set

annotations.note: Expressible as `¬ Convex 𝕜 s` but Mathlib has no dedicated non-convex definition.

annotations.label: Concave polygon

anchor.snippet: A polygon that is not a convex polygon is sometimes called a concave polygon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no general theory of polygons or concave polygons.

annotations.label: Reverse convex set

anchor.snippet: is sometimes called a reverse convex set

annotations.note: No notion of reverse convex set (complement convex) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex combinations stay in S

anchor.snippet: Given r points u 1 , ..., u r in a convex set S

annotations.note: `Convex.sum_mem` shows any finite convex combination of points of a convex set lies in the set.

anchors.snippet: Given r points u 1 , ..., u r in a convex set S

annotations.label: Convex combination

anchor.snippet: is called a convex combination of u 1 , ..., u r

annotations.note: `Finset.centerMass` (with nonneg weights summing to 1) is Mathlib's encoding of a convex combination, also used in `convex_iff_sum_mem`.

annotations.label: Convex hull (intersection)

anchor.snippet: The convex hull of a subset S of a real vector space is defined as the intersection of all convex sets that contain S

annotations.note: `convexHull` is built from `convex_sInter`, i.e. the intersection of all convex supersets.

annotations.label: Convex polytope

anchor.snippet: A (bounded) convex polytope is the convex hull of a finite subset of some Euclidean space

annotations.note: Expressible as `convexHull` of a finite set, but Mathlib has no dedicated convex-polytope type.

annotations.label: Empty set and whole space convex

anchor.snippet: The empty set and the whole space are convex

annotations.note: `convex_empty` and `convex_univ` establish both cases.

annotations.label: Intersection of convex sets is convex

anchor.snippet: The intersection of any collection of convex sets is convex

annotations.note: `convex_sInter` proves the intersection of any family of convex sets is convex.

annotations.label: Union over a chain is convex

anchor.snippet: The union of a collection of convex sets is convex if those sets form a chain

annotations.note: `DirectedOn.convex_sUnion` proves the union is convex for directed families, which generalizes chains.

annotations.label: Closed convex set

anchor.snippet: Closed convex sets are convex sets that contain all their limit points

annotations.note: Closed convex sets are expressed as `Convex ∧ IsClosed`; Mathlib has no bundled closed-convex type but `Convex.closure` shows the closure stays convex.

annotations.label: Closed convex = intersection of half-spaces

anchor.snippet: They can be characterised as the intersections of closed half-spaces

annotations.note: Hahn–Banach separation infrastructure exists, but the explicit characterization as an intersection of closed half-spaces is not stated.

annotations.label: Supporting hyperplane theorem

anchor.snippet: there is a closed half-space H that contains C and not P

annotations.note: Geometric Hahn–Banach gives separation of a closed convex set from an exterior point, but the supporting-hyperplane-at-a-boundary-point statement is not directly proved.

annotations.label: Face of a convex set

anchor.snippet: A face of a convex set

annotations.note: Mathlib's `IsExtreme` (extreme subset) matches the article's notion of a face of a convex set.

annotations.label: Extreme point

anchor.snippet: is a point that is a face of

annotations.note: `Set.extremePoints` defines points lying in no nontrivial open segment within the set.

annotations.label: Compact convex = hull of extreme points

anchor.snippet: that is compact (or equivalently, closed and bounded )

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the closed-convex-hull version (Krein–Milman); the finite-dimensional convex-hull-without-closure statement is not separately given.

annotations.label: Krein–Milman theorem

anchor.snippet: is the closed convex hull of its extreme points (the Krein–Milman theorem )

annotations.note: `closure_convexHull_extremePoints` is precisely the Krein–Milman theorem for compact convex sets.

annotations.label: Faces of a triangle

anchor.snippet: A triangle in the plane (including the region inside) is a compact convex set

annotations.note: The specific enumeration of faces of a triangle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Faces of the closed unit disk

anchor.snippet: The only nontrivial faces of the closed unit disk

annotations.note: `StrictConvexSpace.extremePoints_closedBall_eq_sphere` shows the extreme points of a closed ball are its bounding sphere, generalizing the disk's boundary-point faces.

annotations.label: Convex body

anchor.snippet: Let C be a convex body in the plane (a convex set whose interior is non-empty)

annotations.note: `ConvexBody` is convex, compact and nonempty, differing from the article's convex+nonempty-interior definition.

anchors.snippet: Let C be a convex body in the plane (a convex set whose interior is non-empty)

annotations.label: Inscribed/circumscribed rectangle

anchor.snippet: We can inscribe a rectangle r in C such that a homothetic copy R of r is circumscribed about C

annotations.note: The inscribed/circumscribed rectangle result is not in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: We can inscribe a rectangle r in C such that a homothetic copy R of r is circumscribed about C

annotations.label: Parameterization of planar convex bodies

anchor.snippet: can be parameterized in terms of the convex body diameter D

annotations.note: Parameterization of planar convex bodies via diameter/radius is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: can be parameterized in terms of the convex body diameter D

annotations.label: Blaschke–Santaló diagram

anchor.snippet: is known a ( r , D , R ) Blachke-Santaló diagram

annotations.note: Blaschke–Santaló diagrams are not present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: is known a ( r , D , R ) Blachke-Santaló diagram

annotations.label: Alternative parametrization (width, perimeter, area)

anchor.snippet: can also be parametrized by its width

annotations.note: Alternative width/perimeter/area parametrizations of convex bodies are not formalized.

annotations.label: Closure and interior are convex

anchor.snippet: are both convex (i.e. the closure and interior of convex sets are convex)

annotations.note: `Convex.closure` and `Convex.interior` prove both the closure and interior of a convex set are convex.

annotations.label: Sum scaling of convex set

anchor.snippet: (where

annotations.note: `Convex.add_smul` proves `(a+b)•s = a•s + b•s` for `a,b ≥ 0` on a convex set.

annotations.label: Algebraic interior relations

anchor.snippet: is the algebraic interior of C

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of algebraic interior (core) of a convex set.

annotations.label: Smallest containing convex set

anchor.snippet: is contained within a smallest convex set (called the convex hull of A )

annotations.note: `subset_convexHull` and `convexHull_min` together show the convex hull is the smallest convex superset.

annotations.label: Conv is a closure operator

anchor.snippet: has the characteristic properties of a closure operator

annotations.note: `convexHull` is literally defined as a `ClosureOperator (Set E)`.

annotations.label: Convex sets form a lattice

anchor.snippet: is needed for the set of convex sets to form a lattice

annotations.note: The `ClosureOperator` structure of `convexHull` induces a lattice on convex sets, but no explicit lattice instance is registered.

anchors.snippet: is needed for the set of convex sets to form a lattice

annotations.label: Convex subsets form a complete lattice

anchor.snippet: form a complete lattice

annotations.note: Closed elements of the `convexHull` closure operator form a complete lattice, but Mathlib has no dedicated `CompleteLattice` instance on convex sets.

annotations.label: Minkowski sum of two sets

anchor.snippet: the Minkowski sum of two (non-empty) sets

annotations.note: Pointwise `Set.add` (`s + t = {x+y | x∈s, y∈t}`) is exactly the Minkowski sum.

anchors.snippet: the Minkowski sum of two (non-empty) sets

annotations.label: Minkowski sum of a finite family

anchor.snippet: the Minkowski sum of a finite family of (non-empty) sets

annotations.note: The pointwise additive monoid on `Set` lets a finite Minkowski sum be written as `∑ i, s i`.

anchors.snippet: the Minkowski sum of a finite family of (non-empty) sets

annotations.label: Zero set is the identity element

anchor.snippet: is the identity element of Minkowski addition

annotations.note: In the pointwise `Set` additive monoid the identity is `0 = {0}`, giving `zero_add`/`add_zero`.

anchors.snippet: is the identity element of Minkowski addition

annotations.label: Convex hull of Minkowski sum

anchor.snippet: the convex hull of their Minkowski sum is the Minkowski sum of their convex hulls

annotations.note: `convexHull_add` proves `convexHull (s + t) = convexHull s + convexHull t`.

anchors.snippet: the convex hull of their Minkowski sum is the Minkowski sum of their convex hulls

annotations.label: Finite-collection version

anchor.snippet: This result holds more generally for each finite collection of non-empty sets

annotations.note: `convexHull_sum` extends the commutation to a finite indexed family of sets.

anchors.snippet: This result holds more generally for each finite collection of non-empty sets

annotations.label: Minkowski sum and convex hull commute

anchor.snippet: the operations of Minkowski summation and of forming convex hulls are commuting operations

annotations.note: `convexHullAddMonoidHom` packages `convexHull` as an additive monoid hom, encoding that convex hull and Minkowski addition commute.

annotations.label: Sum of two compact convex sets is compact

anchor.snippet: The Minkowski sum of two compact convex sets is compact

annotations.note: `IsCompact.add` proves the Minkowski sum of any two compact sets is compact (no convexity needed).

annotations.label: Compact + closed convex is closed

anchor.snippet: The sum of a compact convex set and a closed convex set is closed

annotations.note: `IsClosed.add_left_of_isCompact` proves the sum of a compact set and a closed set is closed, generalizing the convex case.

annotations.label: Recession cone

anchor.snippet: the concept of a recession cone of a non-empty convex subset S

annotations.note: Mathlib's `asymptoticCone` corresponds to the recession cone for closed convex sets but is defined via filters rather than as the textbook recession cone.

anchors.snippet: the concept of a recession cone of a non-empty convex subset S

annotations.label: Dieudonné's theorem

anchor.snippet: Let A and B be non-empty, closed, and convex subsets of a locally convex topological vector space

annotations.note: Dieudonné's theorem on closedness of differences of convex sets is not formalized.

annotations.label: Star-convex set

anchor.snippet: C is star convex (star-shaped) if there exists an x 0 in C

annotations.note: `StarConvex 𝕜 x s` states every segment from `x` to a point of `s` stays in `s`.

annotations.label: Convex implies star-convex

anchor.snippet: a non-empty convex set is always star-convex but a star-convex set is not always convex

annotations.note: `Convex.starConvex` shows a convex set is star-convex at each of its points.

annotations.label: Orthogonally convex set

anchor.snippet: is called orthogonally convex or ortho-convex

annotations.note: Orthogonal convexity is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Intersection of orthoconvex sets is orthoconvex

anchor.snippet: an intersection of any collection of orthoconvex sets is orthoconvex

annotations.note: No orthogonal-convexity theory exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesically convex set

anchor.snippet: a geodesically convex set to be one that contains the geodesics joining any two points in the set

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of geodesic convexity in Riemannian/metric geometry.

annotations.label: Convex set in order topology

anchor.snippet: The subspace Y is a convex set if for each pair of points a , b in Y

annotations.note: `Set.OrdConnected` is exactly the order-theoretic convexity: `[a,b] ⊆ s` whenever `a,b ∈ s`.

annotations.label: Convex but disconnected subset of Z

anchor.snippet: a counter-example is given by the subspace {1,2,3} in Z

annotations.note: This specific order-convex-but-disconnected example in ℤ is not stated as a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Convexity (axioms)

anchor.snippet: a convexity over X is a collection 𝒞 of subsets of X satisfying the following axioms

annotations.note: Abstract convexity (a family of subsets closed under intersection and directed union) is not axiomatized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convexity space

anchor.snippet: the pair ( X , 𝒞 ) is called a convexity space

annotations.note: The notion of an abstract convexity space is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Convex space

anchor.snippet: a space is convex if it is possible to take convex combinations of points

annotations.note: `Convexity.ConvexSpace` axiomatizes spaces supporting (finite) convex combinations of points.
